title: START_NOT_STICKY is the correct service restart policy for this use case
description: START_STICKY would cause Android to phantom-restart the foreground service after the process is killed, producing a persistent 'nie relay active' notification with no live WebSocket behind it. START_NOT_STICKY is correct because the relay lives in the Dart isolate: when the user brings the app back, RelayService.connect() is called and start() is triggered from there. A future contributor might see START_NOT_STICKY and try to 'fix' it to STICKY for reliability. Resist this — STICKY produces a worse user experience (stale notification, no actual connection) and the Dart-side reconnect loop already handles restart correctly.
close_reason: Decision recorded during review; no code change required
